stem: A linear search is being used to find the value 7 in the list [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6, 7, 5].
model_answer: Start at the first element. Check whether it equals 7; if so, stop and return its position. Otherwise move to the next element. Continue until either a match is found or every element has been checked. For this list the search compares 3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6, 7 — the ninth comparison matches, so the search returns position 9 (or index 8 if 0-based).
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for: pointer at the start, equality check, advance if no match, terminate at match or end of list. Either 0- or 1-indexed positions are fine.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe, using numbered steps, how a linear search would find the value 7 in the list above. Show how many comparisons are made.

mark_points.text: starts at the first element / first index
compares each element with the target value 7 in order
stops when a match is found OR when every element has been checked
states the answer reaches 7 after 9 comparisons (or position 9 / index 8)
